turns.user_message: I tried following that whole morning routine everyone talks about, thinking it would finally shift my mood, but it just made everything feel heavier. I spent an hour making everything look perfect, only to realize I was just pretending to be someone I am not. It is exhausting to put on this little show of calm when I know deep down I am just going through the motions. The whole thing just reminded me how disconnected I feel from my own life. I guess I just expected a checklist to magically fix how I see myself.

It keeps happening no matter what I try to change, and I am starting to see a really frustrating pattern. Every time I set out to do something nice for myself, I end up picking it apart until the whole effort feels pointless. I catch myself waiting for the other shoe to drop, convinced that any small win is just a fluke. It is like I am fundamentally wired to ruin my own good days before they even really start. I just wish I could experience a moment of peace without immediately questioning if I deserve it.

I am completely drained from constantly trying to manufacture a better version of myself, and it feels like I am just running in place. No matter how many times I reset or start over, the same heavy doubt always creeps back in to take over. I look at all these little efforts I make and they just feel like cheap bandages over something that is completely broken. It is terrifying to think that this hollow feeling is just my permanent baseline, no matter how hard I push. I am so tired of being my own worst critic and watching every attempt at happiness fall flat.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with a failed attempt to feel better.
